content: Just as human language learners use linguistic reflection (the lexical sense of Reflexion) to reinforce iterative feedback loops when refining writing craft, LLMs can integrate lexical-level semantic self-memory from Reflexion agents to better ground Self-Refine’s iterative improvement steps for generation tasks. This hybrid framework would turn abstract self-feedback into concrete, lexically anchored memory traces that prevent the model from repeating the same avoidable errors across refinement iterations.
